question_text: A light ray hits a plane mirror at an angle of 30° to the normal line. At what angle to the normal will the reflected ray leave the mirror?
explanation: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection, both measured from the normal line (the perpendicular to the surface). Since the incoming ray hits at 30° to the normal, the reflected ray will also leave at 30° to the normal, but on the opposite side.
